title: elaeocarpus aristatus
intro: Elaeocarpus aristatus, also known as the Blue Marble tree, is a species of flowering plant native to tropical forests in Asia and the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Elaeocarpus aristatus include reducing fever, relieving pain and swelling, and supporting the immune system. Elaeocarpus aristatus can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat a range of ailments, from respiratory infections to skin conditions.
